title: dactylopsis digitata subsp. littlewoodii
intro: Dactylopsis digitata subsp. littlewoodii is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the 'Tongue Plant' due to its unique leaf shape resembling a tongue. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Dactylopsis digitata subsp. littlewoodii include the treatment of various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
